meaning: adj.[医]不宜动手术的,手术不能治愈的
examples: The policy was thought to be inoperable.
He was diagnosed with inoperable lung cancer.
Failures at this stage will leave the application inoperable.
It was a comedy of errors that ended up with three of four cars temporarily inoperable.
Having lost one eye as a child, he suffers inoperable cataracts on the other.
